How to Change a Blanket Sleeper on an Infant or Child
A blanket sleeper is a type of baby clothing that covers the child's body and legs. It is designed to keep the baby warm and cozy, and is typically used for infants and young children. This article will teach you how to change a blanket sleeper on an infant or child, as well as how to put on a new one.

Changing a Blanket Sleeper
1. Remove the Old Blanket Sleeper
To change a blanket sleeper, first remove the old one by gently pulling it off of the child's body. Try to avoid any sudden movements that could startle the baby.
2. Put on a New Blanket Sleeper
Once the old blanket sleeper has been removed, put on a new one by carefully slipping it over the child's head and arms. Make sure that the new blanket sleeper is properly aligned with the child's body, and that it is not too tight or too loose.
3. Check for Proper Fit
After the new blanket sleeper has been put on, check to make sure that it fits properly and is comfortable for the child. Adjust it as necessary, and make sure that it is not too tight or too loose.

How to Disable Write Protection

Write protection is a feature found on many removable storage devices, such as SD cards and USB flash drives. It is designed to prevent data from being overwritten or erased. If you don't want to worry about accidentally deleting or overwriting your data, you can enable write protection. If you need to disable it to save new data to the device, you can do so as well.

Understand write protection.

Write protection is a feature found on some removable storage devices, such as SD cards and USB flash drives. It is designed to prevent data from being overwritten or erased. If you enable write protection, you will no longer be able to modify or delete any of the data on the device.

If you don't want to worry about accidentally deleting or overwriting your data, you can enable write protection. If you need to disable it to save new data to the device, you can do so as well.

Disable write protection.

If you need to save new data to the device, you will need to disable write protection. To do this, you will need to use a third-party software program or a command prompt.

Use a third-party software program.

There are many third-party software programs that can be used to disable write protection on removable storage devices. Some of these programs are free to use, while others may require a purchase or subscription.

Use a command prompt.

You can also use a command prompt to disable write protection on removable storage devices. To do this, you will need to open a command prompt window and use the following command:

STORAGE DEVICE hanged with "disable write protection" in the directory you want to disable write protection.

Replace "STORAGE DEVICE" with the actual name of your removable storage device, and press Enter to execute the command. This will disable write protection on the device.

How to Paint a Flower on Rice Paper Using Watercolors

Rice paper is a lightweight paper that's made from — you guessed it — rice. Because it's slightly textured, it's a great surface for watercolors. In this tutorial, we'll show you how to paint a flower on rice paper using watercolors.

 Materials
rice paper
watercolors
paintbrushes
ink
piece of cardboard
brown marker

We'll be using the "wet on wet" painting technique. This means that we'll be painting on a surface that's already wet. So, we'll need to prep our work area before we start.

First, cut a piece of cardboard to the size of your rice paper. Then, tape the rice paper to one side of the cardboard. This will give your work surface some weight and stability.

Next, use a brown marker to draw a flower shape on the rice paper. We'll be painting over the marker lines, so don't worry about making them perfect.

Now it's time to start painting!

Pour a small amount of water into a bowl or cup. Dip your paintbrush into the water and then dab it onto the rice paper to wet the area around the flower.

Next, add a small amount of watercolor paint to the end of your paintbrush. Gently dab the paint onto the rice paper, using a light touch.

As you add more paint, the area will become more saturated. At this point, you can start adding more detail to your flower, such as shading and highlights.

When you're happy with the way your flower looks, it's time to add some finishing touches. Use a darker shade of watercolor paint to add some shadows around the edges of your flower. This will help to give it more dimension.

Finally, use a white gel pen or white paint to add some highlights to the center of your flower. This will help to make it pop off the page.

How to Avoid Online Work-at-Home Scams

There are many ways to avoid online work-at-home scams. Be wary of anything that sounds too good to be true, such as ads that promise you can "make money fast" or "get rich quick." These are often the most obvious scams.

Another red flag is if the company requires you to pay a fee upfront. This is something you should never have to do, as it's the company's job to pay you for your work. If they want you to pay a fee, it's likely a scam.

It's also important to research the company before you start working for them. Check online reviews and see what other people have said about the company. If there are many negative reviews, it's probably best to avoid that company.

Finally, be sure to keep your guard up even after you start working. Scammers can sometimes take advantage of people who have already been scammed by getting them to pay more money or sign up for more work. If something seems fishy, it probably is.

Overall, the best way to avoid online work-at-home scams is to be cautious and do your research before signing up for any work or paying any fees.

How to Prepare a Resume for a Transcriptionist

A transcriptionist is a person who listens to audio files and types out what they hear. This is usually done for medical or legal reasons, such as when doctors need to keep a record of their patients' conditions. Transcriptionists can also be used to help with voice recognition software development by transcribing spoken language into written form so it can be used as data for training purposes.

When you're looking for a job as a transcriptionist, your resume is the first thing that potential employers will see. It's important to make a good impression with your resume, as it can be the difference between getting an interview and being passed over for someone else. Here are some tips on how to prepare a resume that will make you stand out from the competition:

1. Start with a strong resume summary. Your resume summary is the first thing potential employers will read, so it's important to make it count. Use this section to highlight your most relevant skills and experience, and to make it clear why you're the best candidate for the job.

2. Use clear and concise language. When you're writing your resume, it's important to use clear and concise language. This will help potential employers quickly scan your resume and understand your qualifications.

3. Make sure your resume is well-organized. A well-organized resume is easier for potential employers to read and will make you look more professional. Use clear headings and bullet points to break up your resume into easy-to-read sections.

4. Include all relevant experience. When you're writing your resume, be sure to include any relevant experience you have. This includes any experience you have working with transcription equipment or software, as well as any experience you have working in a medical or legal setting.

5. Proofread your resume. Before you send your resume to potential employers, be sure to proofread it for any errors or typos. This will help you make a good impression and avoid any unnecessary distractions.

Downloading PS3 Games
The days of PS3s being out of stock and hard to find are long gone, and with them, the days of PS3 game manuals and discs. In their place are digital game downloads, available from the PlayStation Store. As long as you have an Internet connection, you can easily download any game you want.

Prepare your PS3.
Connect your PS3 to the Internet. The PlayStation Store is entirely Internet-based, so you won't be able to download games without it. Make sure your PS3 is connected via either WiFi or Ethernet cable.

Turn on your PS3.
When the PlayStation 3 home screen appears, press the "PlayStation Store" icon.

Browse for games.
Press the "Browse" icon at the bottom of the screen to get to the Browse screen. On this screen, you'll see rows of icons for various games. You can either look for a specific game, or just browse around. 

Downloading a game.
When you find a game you want to download, highlight it and press the "Download" icon at the bottom of the screen. You can either download the game right then or there, or add it to your queue to download later. Adding a game to your queue will save the game to your "Download List," which you can access by going to the "Downloads" icon at the bottom of the PlayStation Store screen.

How to Fill Out a US 1040A Tax Return

The 1040A is one of the simplest tax forms that the IRS offers. It's known as the "short form" and is intended for use by taxpayers whose incomes are less than $100,000. If you're filing as a single taxpayer, you can make up to $85,000 per year and still qualify to use the 1040A. If you're married and filing jointly, you can make up to $100,000.

To get started, the IRS recommends gathering all of your W-2s, 1099s, and receipts for large purchases. You'll need this information to complete the return.

You can get a copy of the 1040A form on the IRS website or by picking one up from your local IRS office.

The form will walk you through entering your income, adjusting your income for things like retirement savings and student loans, and calculating your taxes.

The 1040A has a standard deduction of $12,400 for single filers and $24,800 for married couples filing jointly.1 You can also claim various other deductions and credits, such as the student loan interest deduction, retirement savings contributions, and child tax credits.

Once you've entered all of your information, you can see how much you owe or how much of a refund you'll receive. You can then file the return with the IRS.

How to Start a Blog

A blog can be a public diary, a way to make money, a portfolio, or a landing page for your business. In this article, we'll cover the steps you need to start a blog, from choosing a domain name to setting up your design.

Choose a domain name.
A domain name is the web address that people will type in to access your blog. It's important to choose a domain name that's easy to remember and relevant to the topic of your blog. You can purchase a domain name from a domain name registrar or hosting company.

Set up your hosting.
Hosting is the server on which your blog will live. You'll need to choose a hosting company and sign up for an account.

Install WordPress.
WordPress is a popular blogging platform that makes it easy to create and manage a blog. Most hosting companies offer an easy way to install WordPress, so you'll be able to get your blog up and running in no time.

Choose a theme.
A theme is a template that will determine the design and layout of your blog. There are many free and premium themes available, so you can choose one that fits your style and budget.

Add content.
Once you have your blog set up, it's time to start adding content. Write blog posts that are interesting and relevant to your audience. Use images, videos, and other media to enhance your content.

How to Look Carefree for Summer

Looking carefree is all about finding the perfect balance between looking put together and looking like you aren't trying too hard. This look is all about simplicity and is perfect for those days when you want to look good but not too good. When you're looking for a more casual look, it's important to avoid anything too fussy or over-the-top. Instead, focus on pieces that are comfortable and easy to wear. Here are a few tips for putting together a carefree summer look.

1. Start with a simple base. A pair of jeans or a casual dress is a great way to start a carefree look. Just make sure to avoid anything too tight or restrictive.

2. Choose a comfortable top. A loose-fitting top or a simple T-shirt is a great choice for a carefree look. Just make sure to avoid anything too revealing or over-the-top.

3. Accessorize with care. A simple necklace or a pair of earrings is a great way to add a touch of polish to a carefree look. Just make sure to avoid anything too flashy or expensive-looking.

4. Finish with a casual shoe. A pair of flats or a casual sandal is a great choice for a carefree look. Just make sure to avoid anything too high or formal.

With these tips, you'll be able to create a look that's both comfortable and stylish.

How to Install Subwoofers

Subwoofers are a great way to improve your car audio system. They can provide low-frequency sounds that your regular speakers just can't produce. However, installing them can be a bit tricky, so we've put together this guide to help you out.

Before you begin, you'll need to decide where you want to install the subwoofer. Many people choose to install them in the trunk of their car, but you can also install them under a seat or in a spare tire well.

Once you've decided on the location, you'll need to measure the space to see if it will fit the subwoofer. Most subwoofers are between 8 and 12 inches in diameter, so you'll need to make sure there's enough room for it to fit.

After you've confirmed the size, you can start by removing the subwoofer from its box. You'll need to unscrew it from the bottom and then lift it out.

Next, you'll need to find the mounting holes on the subwoofer. These are usually located on the bottom or sides of the speaker. Once you've found them, you can use a drill to make holes in the location you've chosen.

After the holes are drilled, you can start by threading the speaker wire through them. Make sure the wire is secure and not too loose.

Once the wire is in place, you can start by screwing the subwoofer into place. Make sure it's tight and secure.

Finally, you can connect the speaker wire to the amplifier or stereo. Make sure the connections are secure and not loose.

Now that the subwoofer is installed, you can start by adjusting the settings on your amplifier or stereo. This will allow you to fine-tune the sound so that it's just right.

How to Make a 3D Dinosaur Head

A 3D dinosaur head is a great way to decorate a wall or shelf in a child's room. You can also use it as a cake topper, if you are making a dinosaur-themed cake. This project is best for children in grades three through five, as it involves some sewing and other intricate tasks.

 Gather your materials. You will need a piece of foam, a permanent marker, a pair of scissors, a needle, some thread, and some polyester fiberfill.

 Draw a dinosaur head on the foam. Draw the head as large as you want it to be. Make sure that the shape is proportional.

 Cut out the head. Use a pair of scissors to cut out the shape that you drew.

 Sew the head. Use a needle and thread to sew a simple running stitch around the edge of the head. This will give the head a more finished look.

 Stuff the head. Use polyester fiberfill to stuff the head. Be sure to stuff it firmly, so that it will hold its shape.

 Draw the details. Use a permanent marker to add details such as the eyes, nose, and mouth. You can also use felt or other materials to add more detail.

 Display the head. You can display the head on a shelf or wall, or use it as a cake topper.

How to Combat Loneliness

Loneliness is a feeling of being alone, even when you're surrounded by people. It can be caused by many factors, such as losing a loved one, moving to a new city, or simply not having anyone to talk to. However, there are many ways to combat loneliness and find ways to be happy again.

Find ways to connect with others. There are many ways to do this, such as joining a local sports team or volunteer group, or finding a hobby that you can do with others. You can also try reaching out to old friends or family members who you haven't talked to in a while.

Try to be more social. This can be difficult if you're not used to being around people, but it's important to make an effort to be more social. You can start by simply striking up conversations with people you meet in everyday life, such as your neighbors or co-workers.

Don't be afraid to ask for help. If you're feeling overwhelmed by loneliness, there are resources available to help. You can reach out to a mental health professional or a support group for help in dealing with your feelings.

Remember, you're not alone in feeling lonely. Many people experience loneliness at some point in their lives. By taking steps to connect with others and be more social, you can help combat feelings of loneliness and find ways to be happy again.

Calorimeters are devices that measure the heat of combustion. They are used in many physics experiments, and are also used in some types of engineering. Many different types of calorimeters exist, but all of them operate on the same basic principle. In this article, we'll look at how to build a basic calorimeter.

The first step is to gather the materials you'll need. You'll need a container, a lid, some insulation, and a thermometer. The container can be made of any material, but it's important to make sure that it's strong enough to hold the combustion sample. The lid should also be made of a strong material, and it should fit tightly on the container. The insulation is used to keep the heat of the combustion sample from spreading to the outside environment. The thermometer is used to measure the temperature of the combustion sample.

The next step is to prepare the calorimeter. First, you'll need to fill the container with water. The water should be at room temperature, and it should be deep enough to cover the combustion sample. Next, you'll need to add the combustion sample to the water. Make sure that the sample is completely submerged in the water. Finally, you'll need to add the lid to the container and insulate it.

Once the calorimeter is prepared, you can start the experiment. First, you'll need to light the combustion sample. Then, you'll need to measure the temperature of the combustion sample over time. You should measure the temperature every few minutes for at least 30 minutes.

After you've finished measuring the temperature, you can stop the experiment. To do this, you'll need to remove the combustion sample from the water. Once the sample is removed, you can measure the temperature of the water. This will give you the final temperature of the combustion sample.

Beat the Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe Story Modes

Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe is a fighting game developed by NetherRealm Studios and published by Warner Bros. Interactive Entertainment. The game was released for the PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360 in November 2008.

Story mode in Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe is a single-player mode that allows players to experience the game's story through a series of fights against AI opponents. The story is set in a fictional universe where the DC heroes and villains are pitted against the Mortal Kombat characters in a battle to determine the fate of the world.

To beat the story modes in Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe, you will need to have a good understanding of the game's fighting mechanics and be able to execute a variety of moves and combinations with precision. Here are some tips to help you beat the story modes:

1. Learn the basics: Before you start the story modes, it's important to have a good understanding of the game's fighting mechanics. Take some time to practice the basic moves and combinations in the training mode to get a feel for the controls and how to execute moves with precision.

2. Focus on one character: Choose one character to focus on and stick with them throughout the story modes. This will help you become more familiar with their moves and abilities, which will give you an advantage when fighting against AI opponents.

3. Learn the combos: Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe has a variety of combo moves that can deal a lot of damage to your opponent. Take some time to learn the different combo moves for your chosen character, and practice executing them with precision.

4. Use special moves wisely: Special moves are a great way to deal a lot of damage to your opponent, but they have a long recharge time. Use special moves wisely, and save them for when they will be most effective.

5. Pay attention to the story: As you progress through the story modes, you will learn more about the game's story and the motivations of the characters involved. Pay attention to the story elements as they will often give you hints as to how to beat the next fight.

6. Practice makes perfect: As with any fighting game, practice is key to improving your skills. Take some time between fights to review your performance and identify areas where you can improve.

By following these tips and practicing your fighting skills, you should be able to beat the story modes in Mortal Kombat vs. DC Universe with relative ease. Good luck!

A Virgo is a person who is born between August 23 and September 22. They are known for being hard-working, detail-oriented, and loyal. They are also known for being a bit critical and perfectionist.

If you are dating a Virgo, it is important to understand their personality and how to treat them. First, you should be aware that a Virgo is a very organized person. They like to have everything in its place, and they like to plan everything out. This can be a bit overwhelming at first, but it is important to remember that this is just who they are. Once you understand this about them, you will be able to relax and enjoy your time with them.

Second, a Virgo is a very loyal person. They will stick by your side through thick and thin. However, they do not like to be taken advantage of. If you are going to date a Virgo, you must be prepared to give as much as you take.

Finally, a Virgo is a very critical person. They will always be able to find something that is wrong, no matter how perfect you think you are. It is important to remember that this is just who they are, and you should not take it too personally.

How to Prevent Calls from Telemarketers (USA)

Introduction

 telemarketer calling you? The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has a Do Not Call list, but telemarketers are notoriously Ten seconds into the conversation, you realize it's a telemarketer. Not only are these calls annoying, but they can also be dangerous. Some telemarketers are guilty of vishing, or voice phishing, which is when a scammer uses social engineering to get you to give up personal or financial information.

There are a few things you can do to minimize the number of telemarketing calls you receive. The best way to stop telemarketers is to register for the FTC's Do Not Call list.

FTC's Do Not Call List

The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has a National Do Not Call (DNC) list, which is a database of phone numbers that telemarketers are legally required to search before making a sales call. You can register your phone number with the FTC's DNC list for free.

To register your phone number with the FTC's DNC list, visit donotcall.gov or call 1-888-382-1222. You will need to provide your phone number and agree to receive automated calls or text messages from the FTC.

Once you have registered your phone number with the FTC's DNC list, telemarketers are legally required to search the list before making a sales call. If your phone number is on the list, they are not allowed to call you.

There are a few exceptions to this rule. Non-profit organizations, political groups, and survey researchers are not required to search the DNC list. In addition, if you have recently purchased a product or service from a business, that business is allowed to call you for up to three months after the purchase to offer you additional products or services.

It's important to note that while the FTC's DNC list can stop most telemarketing calls, it won't stop all of them. There are still some telemarketers who find ways to bypass the list. In addition, the DNC list only applies to sales calls. If you are receiving unwanted calls from political groups, non-profit organizations, or survey researchers, you will need to take additional steps to stop these calls.

While getting straight A's without much work is difficult, it is not impossible. Below is a list of steps you can take to improve your chances of getting straight A's without having to work too hard.

Be responsible for your own education.
Don't rely on teachers or parents to tell you what you need to do. Take charge of your own education and set specific goals for yourself.

Set realistic goals.
Do not set goals that are too high or too low. Set goals that are challenging, but also within reach.

Make a plan.
Come up with a plan of action to help you reach your goals. This plan should include specific steps you will take to achieve your goals.

Stay organized.
Keep your workspace and materials organized so that you can find what you need when you need it. This will help you save time and stay focused.

Prioritize your work.
Not all tasks are created equal. Prioritize the tasks that are most important to help you achieve your goals.

Don't be afraid to ask for help.
If you need help, don't be afraid to ask for it. Whether it's from a teacher, parent, or friend, asking for help can be a great way to get the support you need to succeed.

Stay motivated.
Getting straight A's without much work can be challenging, but staying motivated can help you achieve your goals. Find ways to stay motivated, such as setting rewards for yourself when you reach your goals.

Don't be too hard on yourself.
Getting straight A's without much work can be difficult, and it's okay to make mistakes. Don't be too hard on yourself if you don't achieve your goals perfectly. Instead, learn from your mistakes and keep working towards your goals.

The Cobra Pose is a gentle backbend stretch that helps to relieve stress and fatigue that can build up in the body. This stretch also helps to open the lungs and to increase the ability to breathe more fully. The Cobra Pose can be performed by almost everyone, regardless of experience level, and can be modified to provide a more or less intense stretch.

Step 1

Lie flat on the floor on your stomach with your hands placed under your shoulders and your fingers facing forward. Draw your arms in tightly to your chest. Don't allow your elbows to go out to the side.

Step 2

Inhale as you slowly begin to lift your head, shoulders, and upper torso off the floor. Only lift as high as is comfortable for you.

Step 3

Exhale as you hold the stretch.

Step 4

Inhale again as you slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.

Modifications

If this is too intense of a stretch, try placing your hands on a chair or against a wall instead of the floor. Only lift as high as is comfortable for you; you should feel the stretch but not pain. Try to keep your gaze straight ahead as you perform the stretch to help keep your neck aligned.

How to Play Kickball for Adults

Kickball is a game that is easy to learn and can be played by people of all ages. However, playing it as an adult makes it more intense and can be a great workout. Here is how you can play kickball as an adult:

1. Determine the field boundaries.
2. Split into teams.
3. Kick off the game.
4. Kick the ball.
5. Field the ball.
6. Record the score.
7. Win the game.

Let's take a closer look at these steps so you know exactly how to play kickball for adults.

1. Determine the field boundaries
You can play kickball on a baseball/softball field, a football field, or a soccer field. However, if you are playing a pick-up game in a park, you should mark out the field boundaries with some chalk or a paint marker.

The field should be divided into 3 bases, a home plate, and a kickball field.

2. Split into teams
You will need at least 4 players to have a game of kickball. However, the maximum number of players will depend on the size of the field. A team can have up to 15 players.

It doesn't matter if you have more players than this, as long as you have at least 4 players per team. If you have more players, you can always play with more than 2 teams.

3. Kick off the game
The kickball game starts with a coin toss to determine who will be kicking first. The team that wins the coin toss will choose whether to kick first or receive first.

The ball is placed on the kicking team's 10-foot line, which is the line that is 10 feet in front of the home plate.

4. Kick the ball
The pitcher of the kicking team will then roll or bounce the ball to the kicker.

The kicker will then kick the ball with their foot and try to get it past the 4th base of the opposing team.

5. Field the ball
The fielders from the receiving team will try to catch the ball before it hits the ground. If they catch it, they can tag the runner out.

If they don't catch it, they can still throw the ball to the base the runner is heading to. If the ball reaches the base before the runner, they will be out.

6. Record the score
Once the ball has been kicked and the runners have been fielded, you can record the score.

If the ball lands in the designated kickball field, then the runner will be given 3 points. If the ball lands outside the field, the runner will be given 1 point.

7. Win the game
The team with the most points will win the game. If the teams are tied, then you will need to determine a winner with a tie-breaking kick.

The teams will then switch sides and kickers. The first team to score a point will win the game.

BBQ Chicken

This mouth-watering barbecued chicken recipe is a staple at any summer gathering. The combination of a crisp, golden brown skin and a juicy, tender interior will have your guests coming back for seconds. And don't be afraid to let loose — this recipe is easy to make and perfect for beginners. So, if you're looking for a new go-to dish for your next party, look no further than this BBQ chicken!

Ingredients:

2 to 3 lb (0.9 to 1.4 kg) bone-in chicken breasts or thighs
1/2 cup (115 g) brown sugar
1/3 cup (80 ml) apple cider vinegar
1/3 cup (80 ml) ketchup
2 Tbsp (30 g) yellow mustard
1 Tbsp (15 g) prepared horseradish
1/2 Tbsp (7 g) paprika
1/2 Tbsp (3 g) onion powder
1/2 Tbsp (3 g) garlic powder
1/2 Tbsp (3 g) chili powder
1/2 Tbsp (3 g) black pepper
1/2 Tbsp (3 g) cayenne pepper

Instructions:

1. Make the BBQ sauce. In a small bowl, whisk together the brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, ketchup, mustard, and horseradish. Set aside.
2. Prepare the chicken. Rinse the chicken in cold water and pat dry with a paper towel. This will help the chicken crisp up nicely on the outside. If you're using chicken breasts, cut them in half lengthwise so that they cook evenly.
3. Season the chicken. In a small bowl, mix together the paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, chili powder, black pepper, and cayenne pepper.  Season both sides of the chicken with the spice mixture, coating evenly.
4. Add the chicken to the grill. Place the chicken skin-side up on the grill. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes on each side. The chicken is done when the outside is crisp and golden brown, and the inside is tender and juicy.
5. Rest and serve. Remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes before cutting. This will help the juices stay in the chicken. Serve and enjoy!

How to Know When to Tell Someone You Like Them

Knowing when to tell someone you like them can be tricky. You don't want to risk your friendship by revealing your true feelings too early, but you also don't want to wait too long and miss your chance. So how do you know when the time is right?

There are a few things to consider when deciding whether or not to tell someone you like them. First, it's important to think about how well you know the person. If you've been friends with them for a while, you may be more comfortable telling them how you feel. If you don't know them very well, it might be better to wait until you've spent more time together before making your feelings known.

It's also important to consider the circumstances under which you're considering telling someone you like them. If you're both in a relationship with other people, it might not be the best time to make your move. Similarly, if you're both in a situation where it might not be appropriate to express your feelings, it might be better to wait for a more suitable time.

Finally, it's important to listen to your gut. If you feel like the time is right to tell someone you like them, go for it!

This procedure will prevent cold water from flowing into the water heater and prevent hot water from coming out during the cleaning process.
The water valves consist of a cold water valve that may be colored blue, a hot water valve that may be colored red, and a third main valve that runs the water into your home.

,


The purge valves have small handles that resemble the letter "T."
This procedure is done to relieve any pressure that has built inside the valves and will prevent excess hot water from shooting out and coming into contact with your skin.
There may be pressure when removing the purge port valve caps, so it is extremely important to make sure the hot water valve is completely and accurately shut off for safety purposes.
Handle each cap carefully to make sure the rubber washer sealing discs stay in place, which are needed for your valves to function properly.

,


If the manufacturer of your tankless water heater did not provide you with hosing lines, you can purchase them from any retail store specializing in home repair or water heaters. The hosing lines must be long enough to reach between the water heater and your bucket.
You may need to consult your manual provided by the tankless water heater's manufacturer or contact the manufacturer directly for exact instructions regarding this procedure.
In some cases, this procedure may require you to use a sump pump and connect hoses that will discharge and flush water from the tankless water heater using the cold and hot water valves.

,,


Since your tankless water heater is most likely the source of all your drinking water and bathing water, using chemical cleaning solutions may be extremely harmful to your health.

,


This procedure may take up to 45 minutes.

,,,


Tighten the caps completely and firmly without breaking the rubber sealing discs located inside the caps.

,


This procedure may just require you to rotate and open the cold and hot water valves so they are parallel to the position of the main valve that leads into the house.

,


Continue to run the water until it runs steadily without air escaping.
This procedure may take up to 2 or 3 minutes.

The Green Berets are the US Army's 1st Special Forces, a small, specialized unit that conducts unconventional warfare, typically behind enemy lines. The term "Green Beret" refers to the distinctive green beret that these soldiers wear, as well as the Operational Camouflage Pattern (OCP) utility uniform they often wear.

The 1st Special Forces was founded in 1952, and since then has fought in the Vietnam War, the Korean War, and the Gulf War, as well as conflicts in Afghanistan and Iraq. The unit is known for its ability to operate independently and behind enemy lines, and has a reputation for being one of the most elite and secretive units in the US Army.

The Green Berets are considered to be one of the most difficult military units to join, as they require a high level of physical and mental fitness, as well as specialized training and skills. However, for those who are able to meet the rigorous requirements and complete the training, the rewards can be great, as the Green Berets are known for their professionalism, secrecy, and effectiveness.

The 8.9 magnitude earthquake that struck Japan on March 11, 2011, was one of the most powerful earthquakes to hit the country in recent times. The disaster left over 20,000 people dead or missing and turned over a million people into homeless. The road to recovery will be long, but there are many ways that you can help.

Find a charity to donate to. Many charities are currently working to help those affected by the earthquake. You can find a list of charities here: http://www.google.com/url? sa=t&rct=j&q= &esrc=s&source=web&cd=1&ved=0CCIQFjAA&url=http%3A%2F%2Fhelp-for-japan.com%2Fcharities%2F&ei=5J8fT92NJKibleVGBZcC4DA&usg=AFQjCNFs6Ix2Mf7cR0D7daKc9VJ8g  or http://www.charity Navigator.org/.

Donate money. Although it may not be the most effective way to help, donating money can go a long way to helping victims of the earthquake. Money donations can be used to purchase supplies and help victims in a more efficient way. 

Donate supplies. Supplies, such as food, water, and medical supplies, are in high demand in Japan. If you have extra supplies that you can donate, please do so.

Donate blood. Blood donations are also in high demand in Japan. If you are able to donate blood, please do so.

Find other ways to help. There are many other ways to help earthquake victims in Japan. You can volunteer your time and efforts, or find other ways to raise money or supplies.

Having a sentence or two in your head will help you lead the event in the right direction. Are you planning it to educate your community? Persuade potential donors for funds? To celebrate a specific individual or group of individuals? Get as narrow as possible. Whatever you're doing (be it educating, persuading, celebrating, etc.), why are you doing it?


Think of it as your mission statement. Your framework for success. When you know exactly what you want to do, it's a lot easier to do it!;
, What exactly do you want to accomplish? Not how many people do you want to show up, not the actual fact that the event is even happening -- what do you want to come out of this? 5 people to walk away a new part of your organization? $1,000 raised? Minds changed? People excited?


Think of the top three things you wish would happen as a result of this event and concentrate on them becoming a reality. Maybe one goal is financial, one is social, and one is personal. It's up to you!

, Good team members with different skills are a necessity. They can help with everything from preparing schedules and budgets, to making invites and posters, to welcoming in guests and doing the dirty work of cleaning up afterward. In other words, they can help you get things done. And if you can, get volunteers who you trust!


Make sure to keep team members and supervisors "up to speed" with your plans. Collaboration can make your job easier. When you ask them to help, be as complete as possible from the get-go about the expectations for them and their level of involvement.
If you aren't in a situation where finding volunteers is feasible, hire a crew! It all depends on the type of event you're organising. The venue may be able to provide you with one or you can go through a third-party staffing agency.

, All possible expenses, incomes, sponsors, and contingent expenses should be included. If you don't budget, you'll end up with a wad of receipts, an empty pocketbook, and no idea what the heck just happened. Be realistic from day one so no surprises are seen the day of!


Find ways to keep costs low. Can you get volunteers to work for free? Consider cheaper venues (like someone's home)? Remember: a small, simple gathering that goes well is always more impressive than a party thrown to the nines that's a flop.

, This is the most important thing when it comes to your event. What time and what place will make people say, "Yeah, I'll go to that!"? You want a time when everyone will be free and a place that's a convenient location. And something you can afford to book!


Check your community's calendar and consider your audience. If you're working with a bunch of stay-at-home moms, during the day and in the neighborhood is your best bet (maybe with some babysitting offered, too?) If you're channeling students, do a weeknight downtown. If you can, go where they already are.
Of course, certain venues need booking. Contact the place you're thinking about using as soon as possible. They may be busier than you are!

, Logistics for everything. What will parking be like? What about handicap accessibility? What can you do with the size of your space? What equipment will you need? What extra items (drinking water for speakers, badges, brochures) will you need that will be extra expenses? How many people are needed to truly make it run smoothly?


It's important to sit down for a minute with yourself (and with your team) and consider all sides of the story. Is there any obstacle that can be foreseen and prevented? Any special guests that need to be accommodated? Any exceptions that need to be made?

, While you're on the roll that you are, prepare a draft poster. It should include the tentative date, time, venue, chief guest, name of the event, and a theme or tag-line for the event. Since it's so early, you can take your time on this -- but it's best to whip up a preliminary version now to see how it's all coming together!


Think about other ways to get the word out, too. E-mail blasts? Snail mail? Facebook, Twitter, the other two dozen event websites out there (more on that in a bit)? What do you need before the event to get people there and what do you need at the event to keep them?

, It's very possible you feel like a ferret with its head cut off right about now. Take a breath and open up Excel. Prepare a draft schedule of the activities in the event. Make a few spreadsheets to organize your thoughts. It may seem like needless paperwork now, but in two months you'll be profusely thanking your past self for looking out for you.


Prepare a time line (with deadlines) for each activity. Write down everyone's names and where they're needed when. That way you can organize yourself and field any future questions from others.

, Fix the date, venue, (chief guest), staff, name of the event and tag-line. Is there anything that could go wrong? Any last minute switch-ups that could present themselves? At this juncture, everything should be written in permanent ink.

, Get approval for your budget, schedule, etc., from team members as well as supervisors. Right now is a good time to get all the questions sussed out. Does everyone know their obligations? Are they comfortable with any and all issues?


Once again, meet with team members and volunteers to brainstorm any problems that might arise. This is also the perfect time to create an action plan.
Make sure the team does not have any internal problems. Stay in touch with all team leaders as well as team members and volunteers.

, If the event is a mega-event, let different people coordinate different activities, under the supervision of one person. The team leader should be trusted by the team members.


It's a good idea to have one or two people dedicating their time to meeting and greeting and talking up the event as people filter in and the event gets started. Basically they're a reception committee, boosting morale and letting people know they're in good hands.

, You're probably on Facebook and Twitter, but there's a ton of other websites you can use, too, that can help you get the word out. Eventbrite, Evite and Meetup If you haven't heard of these, do some surfing now! And if you're looking to sell tickets online, Ticketbudis pretty good.


And, of course, your website/blog/Facebook page, if you have one. You can send out reminders, set up pictures, and monitor RSVPs. The more active you are, the more your presence will be known. If you're doing an event based around gardening or home decor or anything else creative, you'll can also use Pinterest.

, There will be a lot of expenses being incurred in the upcoming weeks and you don't want them all to come out of your own pocket! Collect at least some of the money to cover the initial fees -- the venue, equipment, caterers, perhaps? Some of these people/places/things may require payment before the event even starts crawling.


Make sure to have a system for receipts, confirmations, invoices, and general paperwork. You'll need to keep everything and refer to it later, so the more organized you are from the beginning the better. Especially if a company you're working with gives you guff.

, Prepare brochures, release advertisements, inform the media, send mailings, make phone calls, send messages to e-groups, and visit potential participants or sponsors. How else will people know to come? Make sure the information you're putting out there is fully complete, leaving them with only a few questions -- they need some to be intrigued, after all!


Think about your target audience. If you're trying to reach senior citizens, you're not gonna be spending your time sending out well-timed Snapchats. Go to the places and use the tools your audience uses.

, These items might include medals, games, mementos, prizes, or certificates. There are a lot of small bits and doodads that might go unnoticed to the untrained eye, but you know every nook, cranny, and whatever fills it. And don't forget tables, chairs, sound equipment, signs, tablecloths, and all that important, big stuff!


This is another thing you should sit down and think about. Don't stop thinking about it until you've found 5 things you've forgotten about -- everything down to pens, a first-aid kit, batteries, ice, and extension cords. You want to know that whatever happens, you've got it covered.

, Make arrangements for photos and videos. Make arrangements for guests' transportation. Make arrangements for food for the clean-up crew. The list could go on and on, but then you'll never get to organizing your event!


Make arrangements for food and refreshments. This is also a good time to prepare for special accommodation such as for those who have disabilities etc. Check for participants who are vegetarian or have other specific dietary needs. Many event ticketing sites such as the ones listed have options for "custom questions" which will better help you gauge any special needs.
Make arrangements for chairs, tables, backdrops, microphones, speakers, computers, LCD projectors, podiums -- anything that needs to be set up at the venue.

, You'll need all relevant phone numbers, addresses and emails of team members. Also, make a similar contact list for VIPs and suppliers of any goods or services. When someone doesn't show up or is running late, this will be what you refer to.


Let's say the caterers aren't there on time. What do you do? You whip out your handy dandy sheet and give them a call. Oh, they thought you were going to pick up 200 pounds of pulled pork? Well, whatever. You hand the sheet to Stu and he grabs his truck and goes to the address listed on the paper. Crisis averted. And now you know to either never book with them again or be more explicit in your directions!

, Look around the place and evaluate parking, toilets, green rooms, arrangements, various entrances and exits. Look for nearby places where you can take photocopies, make phone calls, and buy any things in case of emergency. Basically, get to know your terrain like the back of your hand.


Talk to the contact person, too. They should have a knowledge of the venue better than anyone else. Are there any issues you should know about? Any time constraints? Doors being locked at certain times? God forbid, scheduled fire alarm checks?

, You got this. It's incredibly important to keep a level head and not get caught up in a mental sweat. You've been preparing for months! Everything will be fine. The calmer you are, the calmer your team will be, and the more smoothly the event will go. Besides, it'll all be over shortly!


Seriously. You got this. You've covered all your bases, you've thought of all the possible problems -- if anything goes wrong, you know how to handle it. And remember: no one's going to blame you. A rowdy guest, bad food -- people know that you don't have control over anything. So relax. You'll be okay.

, Make sure you communicate with everyone on how to reach the venue and the time. The last thing you want is your entire team calling you at once the day of the event wondering where the heck the back door is.


Even if no one is coming out and asking you questions, do your best to gauge their behavior. Does everyone seem okay with their job description? Are people getting along? If not, talk to them and see if you can work out the kinks. Maybe someone would be better suited to a different department or working with other people.

, Create a list of invitees in a spreadsheet and get a tally for heads. For most events, the number of people who RSVP will not represent the number of people who actually show. You could have 50 people who said they'll be there, but end up with 5 or 500. So while you should know this number, also be ready to handle either end of the spectrum!


Remind VIPs about the events while you're at it. You'd be surprised how many people will say, "Oh, that's right. That's tomorrow, isn't it?" With a simple phone call or text, that can be avoided.

, Is the room clean and available? Is all the electronic equipment set up and does it seem to be working? Can you pre-load any equipment, if need be? Do the staff seem reasonably prepared?


Check whether enough people are there to man the event. It's always best to have more than you need, of course. You may need someone to run off to do an emergency errand or take care of a guest or issue you didn't see coming. Or, you know, to fetch you coffee.

, This kit might include a bottle of water, a snack bar, note paper, a pen, a brochure and any information they may need. It is also a good idea to include small mementos. This is a nice touch that really convinces people that this is a well thought-out, organized event. And it makes them feel appreciated!


This can be for guests or it can be for your crew, or both! Who doesn't love a free granola bar and pen?

, This is a list of all the necessary information sorted by time and/or room. Prepare a minute-to-minute agenda for important activities. The format for this is up to you. Just try to keep the amount of information on it to a minimum so it's easy to read.


If you're really type A and super diligent, you may find it useful to make different types of running sheets. Speakers may want a list of other speakers and where they are and at what time. Your crew may just want a list of equipment, times, and clean-up protocol. If you have the time, this may be useful.

, How terrible would it be if you got there, everything's there, everyone is there, and you realize the only thing that's not are the 12000 cups you forgot at your house? Bummer. Now you've just ruined everything. So make your checklist, check it twice, and bring everything that needs to be brought!


If things are half a dozen places, assign people to each specific duty. That way, the day of, you're not spending 8 hours running around collecting everything and freaking out. Spreading the work makes for lighter hands...or however the saying goes.

, Check that everyone is there and all electronic equipment is in working order. Any last minute questions? If there's time, pass out the Mountain Dew, give a pep talk, and break! You got this. You're so prepared.


Make sure the organizers are wearing a distinct badge or some other noticeable implement so that participants can find help if needed. Sometimes khakis just aren't enough.

, Inside and outside. Do you need balloons on the mailbox? A posterboard on the corner? What about on the doors and through the hallway? If your guests have to wander through a veritable labyrinth, the more signs, the better.


Welcome banners and other info in front of the building will be especially useful. You want people to be able to see from the street that that's the place they should be. No questions about it!
Make a reception and registration counter. When guests walk in the door, they should see exactly what they need to do. Otherwise they'll be floating around unsure and ill at ease. And remember that reception committee we talked about? Have someone at the door to welcome them in and answer their questions.
Put on some music! It can kill any awkwardness that otherwise might be flitting about.

, If a speaker is running late, there needs to be stalling. If eating is taking longer than accounted for, they need to be alerted to the schedule changes. Very rarely do events go completely as planned -- so as you deviate from your course, make sure the lines of communication are open.

, You may want something to remember of your own. And seeing someone walking around with a camera can get people excited. Take note of the sponsor banners, your banner, the entrance, receptions, etc. Maybe you can use it for next year!


Have a friend or a professional photographer take care of this, if at all possible. You have enough on your plate! You'll need to be schmoozing and boozing your guests, so have someone else take care of the photo stuff.

, You probably planted something in your guests' heads and want them walking away either thinking something, wondering something, or looking to take action. So have a pamphlet or something they can take with them that they can personally do after the event.


Part of this could be a forum for feedback. Offer them a way to follow up and say what they thought, how would they might make improvements, and what they'd like to see next time. And, of course, how they can get involved!

, Check the electric meter, remove the banners, tables, etc. You want to leave the place as good as when you found it -- especially if you paid for the venue and want to ever come back. They may charge fees that could otherwise be avoided. Divvy up the jobs so it all goes as quick and painlessly as possible.


Check to make sure nothing valuable has been left behind, and if so, form a lost and found.
If you have damaged something, let the venue's contact person know. It's best to be honest and forthright.
Take care of the trash to the best of your ability. Maintenance will take care of everything from there.

, Depending on your event, this could range from absolutely nothing to a long-list of thank yous and receipts. Here are some ideas to get you started:


Thank the all team members, especially sponsors and volunteers. You couldn't have done it without them!
Finalize and settle the accounts. This should be done as quickly as possible. The fewer loose strings, the better.
Have a thank you party for those who helped. You want your crew to feel appreciated and donors to feel like they made a difference to a good cause.
Distribute any souvenirs or other publications to relevant people.
Deliver the receipts to sponsors and others.
Post the photos in your event website.

, After all is said and done, what would you have done differently? What worked and what didn't? Would you ever choose to organize an event like this again? What have you learned?


If you receive feedback, go over it. And if you're not getting it from your guests, ask your crew! What did they think? Did they at least enjoy themselves? It was the free granola bar and pen, wasn't it?

(detailing your partner at least once a year isn't a bad idea either!) Wash the dirt and road salts from the top AND bottom...get underneath there and scrub. Next, wax her down. Vacuuming up any food crumbs will discourage animals (other than yourself) from getting some action in your back seat over the Winter.;
, Ideally, you should drain your car's fluids while it's still warm. When it is heated and circulating, oil picks up and emulsifies contaminants. That's why you must always wear a glove to protect yourself from hot engine oil burns. Note: Make sure the oil contains corrosion protection.

, This only applies if your car has lube fittings. Put clean grease on all of the steering and suspension fittings and pack the front wheel bearings.

, The best way to insure your baby against brake deterioration is to drain your system of old fluids and refill with new ones. (You can apply this one to your partner too...*wink*)

, Once you've done that, start your car to run the rest of the gas out of the lines. Any old gas left behind can clog carburetors and valves. If you are only going to be storing your car for a few months, you can simply add a can of gasoline stabilizer instead.

, This is so air can continue to circulate. Then, refill it with a rust-inhibiting antifreeze. Don't forget to disconnect the heater hoses and drain the heater too. After this, all the car's fluids should be completely drained. BE A FRIEND TO THE ENVIRONMENT: You MUST contact your local household and hazardous waste service for proper disposal!

, Wash it down with water and baking soda, then rinse it off with distilled water. Store it off the ground, and in a dry place...but not on concrete. You can also hook it up to a battery charger/maintainer to keep the battery alive.

, Remove the tires and stack them, placing cardboard between each one as you do so. Finally, cover tires to protect them from harm.

,), Depress the clutch and lock it in position. To do this, press a 2 x 4 against the clutch and the front of the seat. This will prevent your clutch plates from sticking together in storage.

,, Convertible tops can shrink if they are left in the down position for an extended period of time.

, When choosing a car cover, it's very important to select the right kind of fabric. Cotton flannel fabrics breathe well, allowing air to circulate through them as well as being "cottony soft" and gentle on your paint job. On the other hand, cotton/polyester fabric blends trap heat and moisture. Plastic films don't breathe at ALL and, therefore, should be avoided. They are a breeding ground...for rust...not like your car’s back seat, which might be a “Breeding Ground” of another kind.

This way, the toppings will be ready and you can focus on preparing for the party event. Make sure all of your topping items are covered in their serving containers and put them into the refrigerator ahead of time.

Don't prepare foods more than 24 hours ahead of time. Keeping them as fresh as possible is important.;
, Cheeses that melt easily include Cheddar and Monterey Jack, although mozzarella and feta cheeses are popular choices with potatoes as well. Grate the cheese and put into a serving dish. Cover with a lid or kitchen plastic wrap., This is the main decision, as you can offer toppings that accommodate the potato as the main course, or the potato as part of the entrée.

Potato as the main course: Bacon bits, diced pepperoni, diced cooked sausage, or diced ham
Potato as part of the entrée: Chili, fajita meats (such as chicken, beef, or steak), cooked bacon strips, or taco meat.
Cook your bacon, crumb, and put into your serving dish.

, Set up bowls containing diced tomatoes, olives, chopped scallions, or sauteed mushrooms.,

Fill up salt and pepper shakers for convenience.
You might like to use vegan butter, not only to accommodate any non-dairy guests, but it's more nutritional and less fattening than regular butter.Choose light sour cream for additional flavor.
Basil, chives, and oregano are popular herbs to use. Insert labels to avoid mistakes.
Transfer the sour cream to a serving dish.

,, Put soup or stew into the pan or crock pot that you'll be serving it from.

,,, Save some table room for different chips, such as Frito's or a simple fruit salad., Non-proportional and curved potatoes may not bake the same as smooth and small., Use a vegetable scrubber to scrub them under running water.
