How to Eat Healthily at an Italian Restaurant

Italian food is highly regarded as one of the best cuisines in the world, but unfortunately, it has a reputation for being fattening.  Italian restaurants often offer a variety of healthy options, so you can enjoy your favorite Italian dishes without feeling guilty.  By choosing the right foods and watching your portion sizes, you can have a healthy and delicious Italian meal.

Choose vegetables over pasta.  Italian restaurants often offer a variety of vegetable dishes, such as roasted eggplant, broccoli, and mushrooms.  These dishes are typically lower in calories and fat than pasta dishes, so they're a healthier option.

Get sauce on the side.  Sauces can add a lot of extra calories and fat to a dish, so it's best to ask for them on the side.  This way, you can control how much sauce you eat and avoid overindulging.

Select lean proteins.  Italian restaurants often offer a variety of lean protein options, such as grilled chicken, fish, and tofu.  These protein sources are typically lower in calories and fat than more traditional Italian meats, such as sausage and pepperoni.

Avoid fried foods.  Italian restaurants often offer a variety of fried foods, such as calamari and mozzarella sticks.  These foods are typically high in calories and fat, so it's best to avoid them if you're trying to eat healthily.

Watch your portion sizes.  Italian restaurants often serve large portions, so it's easy to overindulge.  Try to stick to one or two courses, and avoid ordering too much food.  This will help you keep your calorie intake under control.

Overall, Italian restaurants can be a great place to enjoy a healthy and delicious meal.  By choosing the right foods and watching your portion sizes, you can have a guilt-free Italian meal that you'll love.

How to Understand the Format of a Yelp Business Page

Yelp business pages provide a wealth of information about local businesses, including the hours of operation, contact information, customer reviews, and other critical bits of knowledge that can help customers learn more about a business and decide whether to visit it.
However, some of this information is only accessible by Yelp users who have signed into their accounts. signed-in users can also post their own reviews, add friends, and send messages to other Yelp users. This article will walk you through the different sections of a Yelp business page so that you can better understand what information is available to the public and what information is only accessible by signed-in Yelp users.

Business Information
The business information section of a Yelp business page includes the business's hours of operation, its physical address, its phone number, and its website address. This information is critical for customers trying to contact or visit the business, so it's important that it be accurate.
However, some businesses choose to hide certain information, like their phone number or address, from the public. If you don't see the information you need in this section, it's possible that the business has hidden it from view. In this case, you'll need to contact the business directly to get the information you need.

Customer Reviews
The customer reviews section of a Yelp business page is one of the most important sections for potential customers. This section contains reviews written by other Yelp users about their experiences with the business.
These reviews can be extremely helpful for potential customers trying to decide whether to visit the business, so it's important that they be honest and accurate. While some businesses choose to hide negative reviews from the public, the vast majority of businesses on Yelp have nothing to hide, and you should be able to find a wide range of reviews in this section.

Business Photos
The business photos section of a Yelp business page contains photos that have been uploaded by the business or its customers. These photos can include anything from pictures of the business's food or products to photos of the business's interior or exterior. These photos can be a great way for potential customers to get a feel for the business before they visit it.

 EVENTS
The events section of a Yelp business page contains information about events that the business is currently hosting or participating in. These events can include anything from a grand opening celebration to a local fair or festival. This section is a great way for businesses to promote upcoming events and for potential customers to learn more about what the business has going on.

Friends
The friends section of a Yelp business page is only accessible by signed-in Yelp users. This section allows Yelp users to add friends and send messages to other Yelp users. It's a great way to stay connected with friends and family who are also Yelp users.

What significantly sets managers apart is the migration away from a concept known as "individual contribution." Managers are not, primarily, individual contributors. That means that you are going to be responsible for the work of others; your success depends on how well your team performs. You are now responsible for far, far more work than you could ever possibly produce by yourself (see warnings). You can’t fix all the problems – don’t even try… that’s not your job anymore.;
, You may have a different dress code to follow. You will have new rules to obey (particularly in the area of Human Resources).



Find a mentor: Not your immediate manager, but find another manager with lots of experience and ask that person to help you transition. This is a very important and often overlooked tool. It will also gain you considerable esteem in the eyes of the upper level management team. It shows maturity.

Join a networking group: There are lots of these (Toastmasters for example). Ask other managers and executives about local clubs. Take advantage of the networking opportunities in your area.

Contact HR: Go to the Human Resources department and ask if there are any HR books or training courses you can use to help you. Read a bit about being a manager. There is a mountain of literature on the subject. Read some of the more well-known books ("The One Minute Manager" and "The Seven Habits of Highly Effective People" are management staples).

Help your staff cope: It's possible the people you will manage used to be your peers and that will be the cause of envy (possibly resentment) and friction. You can't prevent it but if you keep the lines of communication open it will reduce the issues. However, you must remember that you are now management and while you don't want to flaunt it, you can't let your former peers take advantage of your previous relationship. Even if they weren’t your peers, getting a new manager is always disconcerting. Address the staff and let them know your plans. Establish the manager/staff relationship early. Even if it seems a bit awkward at first do not be bashful about it... just follow the steps, be yourself, and don't forget where you started.

Don't neglect family: Your husband-wife-playmate-whatever and children, if any, and friends still need your attention just like they did before. You will have a lot more on your mind now - management is a hard transition. Keep your priorities in check. If you hear people mentioning that you're distant - take note. You don't want to let your career ruin your family relationships (you wouldn't be the first).

Don't neglect your health: OK, you've discovered that this is actually fun. The work is exciting, you're working longer hours, maybe working more at home, staying up a little later, getting up a little earlier, doing a great job handling the family and kids... are you sleeping enough? Are you sure?

, Do you have hourly, daily or weekly targets your team must meet; what about your new goals such as reviewing productivity? Write down everything and post it prominently (see tips). This will be your checklist. A word of caution here, this list will change over time; it is a living document. Some things may stay the same (service levels, for example) but other things may change depending on the strategies handed down to you from the Executive Management suites. Review your list frequently, with a critical eye, and revise it when necessary.

, John works extremely fast but occasionally misses some of the details. Jane is incredibly thorough but has issues with the volume of work processed. Bill has spectacular customer relationships but can never say "no" to the clients whereas Mary has excellent technical skills but falls short in people skills. You really need to know all this very, very well. You will use this knowledge to balance the productivity of your team.

, This is termed skill-based work assignment. You want to play into each person’s strengths and minimize work assignments that would target his or her weaknesses. If you have the opportunity, put people together that have complimentary skill sets. You might put John and Jane on a project together, or have Mary and Bill consult together on a presentation.

, These meetings have several purposes.



Give feedback on job performance: Discuss the previous week’s objectives including what went well, what areas might improve for next time and how that improvement might be obtained. This will lead into…

Outline the goals for the next meeting: These are typically referred to as "action items" and will form the basis for the next weekly production review.

Learn about staff issues: You’re going to be a bit out of touch now and you absolutely must realize that. The only way to keep track of issues that affect the performance of your team (and therefore your job) is to listen to your employees!

Ask for ideas: Your staff wants to feel engaged. Without exception, the number one motivating factor behind people leaving a job is poor management – that very often stems from feeling ignored. You will be graded not only on your team’s performance, but also on your turnover rate.

Motivation: In Peter Scholtes' lectures, he points out that people motivate themselves. The best managers find ways to get their people to become motivated to do their jobs well and with pride. Use these sessions to find out what motivates your employees and use that feedback to improve their contribution.

, Sometimes the initial workload will seem overwhelming and you might have a tendency to sequester yourself away from the staff to keep up – especially with the new paperwork load you’ll have. You absolutely must not give the appearance of being an "ivory tower" inhabitant. If your team members don’t see their leader, they’ll develop an attitude of anarchy. Things will go very badly for you. Even if you’re managing remote staff, you need to make sure they "feel" your presence. If you manage multiple shifts, make sure you visit all shifts regularly.

, This will be especially important if there are significant issues that arise. Problems are expected; how you and your team handle those problems must be a focus of your efforts.

, Recognition is much more effective. If you have the authority, perhaps grant performance leave (an extra day off for doing something exceptional). Make the rewards regular and make them attainable but difficult. When you give a reward, make it well known (reward publicly, admonish privately).

, Learn to do this properly. If you do it correctly, you will get the results you want. If you do it poorly, things can go horribly wrong. See How to Give a Feedback Sandwich for ideas.

,,,,

, This is a free, open-source recording and broadcasting program. It is designed for broadcasting video to others, but can easily be used to capture your screen and computer audio. OBS is available for Windows, Mac, and Linux. You can download it from obsproject.com.

, Run the installer after downloading it. The installation process is very straightforward, and you shouldn't need to change any of the settings.

, You'll be greeted with the main OBS interface. You'll see a large blank frame in the center, and various options along the bottom of the window.

, You'll want to make a few adjustments before you start recording with OBS. You'll only need to do this the first time you use it.

, You'll be able to adjust your recording settings here.

, By default, OBS will save your videos to the Videos folder. You can change this to any folder you'd like by clicking the "Browse" button.

, This will create MP4 files, which are compatible with the most devices.

, This will let you adjust some resolution settings.

, Match it to "Base (Canvas) Resolution". This will ensure that your HD video recordings stay at the highest possible resolution.

, You can set a key to quickly start and stop the recording so that you don't have to switch between the OBS window and the video.

, You can press any combination of keys. Make sure to choose a combination that isn't normally used, like Ctrl+F12.

, You'll see a preview of the capture, which should be a miniature version of your current display.

How to Trek to Triund Hill
Triund Hill is a famous trekking spot in the Dhauladhar range of the Himalayas. A moderately difficult trek, the Triund Hill trek takes you through the scenic beauty of the Himalayan range. The view of the mountains, the nearby glacier, and the lush green forests is a breathtaking experience in itself. The best time to trek to Triund Hill is from March to June and September to December. However, it is possible to trek in the winter months as well.

If you're looking for a challenge, the Triund Hill trek is perfect for you. With its moderate difficulty level, this trek is a great way to get out and explore the beauty of the Himalayas. The view from the top of Triund Hill is breathtaking, and the trek itself is a rewarding experience.

When you're planning your trek to Triund Hill, there are a few things you should keep in mind. First, make sure you're in good physical condition and that you're able to handle the moderate difficulty level of the trek. Second, be sure to pack enough food and water for the journey. And finally, be sure to check the weather conditions before you set out so that you're prepared for any changes that might occur.

So if you're ready for a challenge, the Triund Hill trek is waiting for you. With its beautiful scenery and rewarding experience, it's sure to be an adventure you'll remember for years to come.

Playing drum rudiments is a great way to practice and improve your overall drumming skills. They can help you develop better stick control and coordination, as well as improve your reading skills.

To play a drum rudiment, you will need to start by holding the sticks in the correct position. The right stick should be held in the right hand, and the left stick should be held in the left hand. The sticks should be held so that the tips are pointing down towards the ground, and the hands should be placed so that the right hand is on top of the left hand.

Once you have the sticks in the correct position, you will need to start by playing the rudiment slowly. Start by playing one stroke at a time, and then gradually increase the speed until you can play the rudiment at the desired tempo.

As you are playing the rudiment, you should focus on keeping the sticks in the correct position and hitting the drums in the correct spot. It's also important to make sure that you are playing the rudiment in time with the music.

There are many different drum rudiments that you can play, so you can choose the ones that work best for you. Once you have mastered one rudiment, you can move on to another one.

How to Make Red Reindeer Food

Red reindeer food is a fun holiday treat for kids. This mixture of red-colored sugar and marshmallows looks just like Santa's reindeer food, but is better because it is edible. When the kids are done leaving out cookies and milk for Santa, they can enjoy gobbling up the reindeer food. Because this treat is so simple, kids can help make it.

Pick out the red sugar. Red reindeer food is made with red sugar. You can find red sugar at most craft stores. If you can't find red sugar, you can make your own by adding a few drops of red food coloring to regular white sugar.

The amount of red food coloring you add will determine the intensity of the red color in the sugar. Add 3 to 4 drops for a pale red color or up to 10 drops for a darker red.

Mix in the marshmallows. Toss 1 cup (200 grams) of marshmallows into the red sugar. The marshmallows will quickly become covered in the sugar.

Stir the mixture with a spoon or rubber spatula so that all of the marshmallows are evenly coated.

Use the red reindeer food right away or store it in an airtight container until you are ready to use it.

How to Organize Your Trading Cards

Trading cards are a fun and popular way to collect and trade information about sports, hobbies, and entertainment. If you have a lot of trading cards, though, they can get disorganized quickly. This guide will help you keep your trading cards neat and organized.

Choose how you want to store your trading cards. The most common way to store trading cards is in binders. These binders have pages that hold 9, 12, or 18 cards, and they come in a variety of sizes and styles (including some that are specifically for Pokémon Trading Cards). You can also store your trading cards in a box or other container.

Organize your trading cards by type. If you have a lot of trading cards, it's a good idea to organize them by type. This will make it easier to find specific cards when you need them. You can organize your trading cards by type using binders, boxes, or other containers.

Arrange your trading cards in alphabetical order. If you want to be able to find specific cards quickly, it's a good idea to arrange them in alphabetical order. This will help you find cards more easily, and it will also make your collection look more organized.

Consider buying trading card sleeves. Trading card sleeves are small, clear plastic sleeves that you can put your trading cards in to protect them from damage. They're a good idea if you want to keep your trading cards in good condition.

Don't forget to have fun with your trading cards! Trading cards are meant to be enjoyed, so make sure to take the time to look at and enjoy your collection.

How to Pray the Chaplet of St. Michael

The Chaplet of St. Michael is a powerful devotion for the archangel Michael, the defender of the Church.

When to Pray the Chaplet of St. Michael

You can pray the Chaplet of St. Michael at any time, but it is especially recommended on his feast day, which is May 8.

How to Pray the Chaplet of St. Michael

The Chaplet of St. Michael is a simple devotion that consists of nine beads, which represent the nine days that St. Michael struggled against Satan.

The Chaplet of St. Michael

To begin the Chaplet of St. Michael, you will need to set up your prayer space with the following items:

- A set of nine beads
- A cross or medal of St. Michael
- A candle

Start by holding the cross or medal of St. Michael in your hand and saying a prayer to the archangel, such as the following:

"St. Michael, defend us in battle. Be our protection against the wickedness and snares of the devil. May God rebuke him, we humbly pray, and do thou, O Prince of the heavenly hosts, by the power of God, cast into hell Satan and all the evil spirits who roam about the world seeking the ruin of souls."

Then, move the cross or medal to the first bead and say the following prayer:

"St. Michael, protect us with your wings of courage."

On the second bead, say:

"St. Michael, cover us with your shield of faith."

On the third bead, say:

"St. Michael, vanquish our enemies with your sword of truth."

On the fourth bead, say:

"St. Michael, give us the strength to resist evil."

On the fifth bead, say:

"St. Michael, help us to overcome our fears."

On the sixth bead, say:

"St. Michael, protect us from all harm."

On the seventh bead, say:

"St. Michael, keep us safe from all danger."

On the eighth bead, say:

"St. Michael, guide us to the kingdom of God."

On the ninth bead, say:

"St. Michael, thank you for your protection and for your unceasing help."

After you have finished the Chaplet of St. Michael, you can end your prayer with a final prayer to the archangel, such as the following:

"St. Michael, I thank you for your protection and for your help in all my needs. I ask you to continue to watch over me and all those who call upon your aid. Amen."

How to Install a Subversion Source Control Server on Ubuntu 10.04

Subversion is a popular, open source source code control system. While it is often hosted on dedicated source code control servers, it can also be installed and used on local servers and workstations. This how-to will walk you through the steps to install Subversion on an Ubuntu 10.04 server.

Install the Subversion package

First, you'll need to make sure that all of the necessary packages are installed on the server. The Subversion package requires several dependencies, including the apache2 web server.

1. Open a terminal window on the Ubuntu 10.04 server.

2. Update the package manager's list of available packages by running the following command:

sudo apt-get update

3. Install the Subversion package by running the following command:

sudo apt-get install subversion

4. Install the apache2 web server by running the following command:

sudo apt-get install apache2

5. Start the apache2 web server by running the following command:

sudo /etc/init.d/apache2 start

6. Open a web browser and go to the following address:

https://YourServerIP/svn/

If you receive a page that says "It works!" then you know that Subversion is installed and that apache2 is running.

Set up the Subversion repository

Now that you have Subversion installed, you'll need to create a repository. A repository is a collection of files and folders that are managed by Subversion.

1. Open a terminal window on the Ubuntu 10.04 server.

2. Switch to the root user by running the following command:

sudo su

3. Create a new repository by running the following command:

svnadmin create /svn/YourRepositoryName

4. Grant read and write access to the repository by running the following command:

sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /svn/YourRepositoryName

5. Switch back to the user account that you used to log in to the server by running the following command:

exit

6. Open a web browser and go to the following address:

https://YourServerIP/svn/YourRepositoryName/

If you see a list of files and folders, then you know that the repository has been created successfully.

Reset circuit breaker to "On" or replace any blown fuse(s) and wait 30 - 60 minutes for the water to warm. If water has remained cool, continue troubleshooting steps below.
, Most water heaters are supplied by voltages that can cause shock, burns and even death should an energized conductor come in contact with the body. Shut off power in the electrical panel by removing fuses or by moving the handle of a switch or circuit breaker dedicated to the water heater to the Off position. Completely remove and "pocket" the fuses or otherwise secure or lock the panel and place a note on the cover to alert everyone that work is being performed on the water heater circuit. This will prevent the accidental energizing of the circuit while you are working on it., The metal covers are usually held in place with screws. Remove the screws and save for reinstallation when done. Use a voltmeter or test light to check between wiring terminals and the grounded metal case of the tank to be sure that the power is off. If power is still on, do not proceed until you locate the fuse or circuit breaker supplying power. Lock off or secure the circuit breaker or remove fuses to prevent someone from turning the circuit on while you are working on the water heater., Once the thermal insulation has been removed, plastic shock protectors are visible. Carefully fold any wires away from the protective cover. Lift the tab at the top away from the clip and remove to access terminals.

View with plastic shock protector removed:







, Water heaters can leak as a result of a failing tank - but also due to poorly fitted or soldered cold water supply pipe / hot water output pipe or a poor seal between tank opening and heating element. If allowed to continue to leak, it will cause internal damage to the controls should water enter them.

Rust coats wires and controls - both outside and inside






Rust is conductive - even when on the wire insulation. This can lead to potentially deadly shocks, heating and melting insulation and even burns. Sooty, black carbon deposits on surfaces indicate a short circuit. Chances are there is an exposed copper wire that may be difficult to see due to the carbon deposits that resulted from the short circuit.
Wires may have been damaged and now suffer with a reduced circumference that is needed to safely carry the heating element electrical load. This point of damage also becomes a source of heat. It is very important to repair or replace all parts that have visible forms of water and short circuit damage. This includes wires, wire insulation, jumpers and the controls themselves. As mentioned above, rust is a conductor and provides unintended and unexpected paths for electricity. These unintended paths can be dangerous and make troubleshooting very difficult to perform.
Here, the yellow wire between the control and element appears to have shorted to the tank (or other metal) leaving a sooty black deposit on the wire and above. Notice the lower left terminal of the thermostat. Excessive heat has caused the plastic around the terminal to begin to melt.







, Usually, the top two terminals have wires that go up to the field wiring compartment that bring the power to the rest of the water heater's controls and heating elements. The "upper controls" consist of the High-Temperature Limit Switch and Upper Thermostat. The "lower controls" refers to the Lower Thermostat (there is no High-Temperature Limit Switch for the lower section of most electric water heaters). Three of the four terminals are numbered and visible in the picture (#1, #3, & #4; the #2 terminal is not identified as it is connected directly to the thermostat below via factory installed jumper).







Thermostat: Has a graduated, adjustable dial. The dial may indicate letters "A" "B" "C" etc., "warm, hot and hotter" or as in the case of the one pictured, the actual temperature in degrees. The thermostat is located just below the High Temp Limit Switch.







Heating Element: Has two terminals with a wire connected to each. One of these wires is usually connected to the thermostat associated it (in these photos the thermostat is directly above it). It is usually located below the controls and supports the controls with a clip of some type (in this photo, it has two terminals and a gray metal clip attached to support the controls above).







, Set the meter for AC Volts and insert the black probe into the black or common jack, and the red probe into the red or Volts jack., Set the AC voltage range to the highest available. Touch the probes to the top terminals of the high temperature limit switch as shown in the image to the right. If desired, the range may be lowered to any desired - provided the range is greater than the voltage displayed at the highest range. If unable to prove power off, double check circuit panel. Do not proceed unless power is proved off; otherwise damage to meter is likely and shock or burns is possible in the steps that follow.

In the provided picture, the meter indicates 0.078 volts present. This is less than 1/10 of a volt, and is considered "off".

, Observe the meter indication. If an analog type, the needle or pointer will be resting at over the higher resistance values (left-most position) this is an "open circuit" indication. If digital meter is used, it may display "OL" or "1" ("1" without trailing or leading zeros). This OL or 1 indication represents a value greater than the meter is capable of displaying (similar to the way a calculator does) for "overload" or "infinity". Infinity in high resistance range is also called an "open circuit" or "Open Loop" (OL). Take note of this open circuit indication for this meter (when in a voltage or current range is selected and the meter displays OL or 1, the measurement should be taken again after adjusting the range upward). If you are unsure what your meter should read in an "OL situation", just leave the terminals unconnected to anything and not touching each other and switch the meter on, it should then indicate the resistance of the air between the terminals which should be infinity in normal conditions.,,,, If the meter being used does not provide for range selection, it is likely an "autoranging" type. This simply means that the meter will automatically adjust the range up or down as needed without any intervention by the user. This feature is far more common on digital meters than analog types. Most analog meters that do not provide manual range selection likely support only a single range. These meters are much more accurate indicating low values (such as 0 through 500k or 1M ohms) than higher values (such as those above 1M), but will work well for this procedure. Pay close attention to the display of an auto-ranging digital meter when reading - there is a huge difference between 20, 20K and 20M ohms. A "K" indicates thousands, and an "M" indicates millions. The examples above would correctly be read as 20 ohms, 20,000 ohms (20K ohms or 20 kilo ohms) and 20,000,000 ohms (20 meg ohms or 20 million ohms). Each is 1000 times larger than the previous., The analog meter pointer should move to lowest values of the resistance scale (or fully to the right). The digital meter should indicate 0 or a "very low" value approaching zero. Locate the "Zero Adjust" knob and turn so that the the meter indicates "0" (or as close to "0" as possible). Most digital meters do not have this Zero Adjust feature. Once "zeroed", this needle position on the dial is the "short circuit" or "zero ohms" indication for this range of this meter. The meter must be zeroed if the resistance range is changed. Measured resistance values will be inaccurate if unable to properly zero the meter.

In the example picture, the meter indicates 0.2 ohms resistance - or zero. The meter can not display a value lower than this value, since there is no zero adjust feature it is considered 0.

, If unable to obtain a zero ohm indication on an analog meter, this may mean the batteries are weak and should be replaced. Retry the zeroing step above again with fresh batteries. Digital meters often graphically display the battery's level of discharge or simply an indication to replace the battery. Check the meter manual for help determining the battery charge state., Read the meter display. Look for a range multiplier (a "K" or "M" in the display) to be sure the value displayed is really ohms, not kilo ohms (K) or meg ohms (M).

In the provided picture, the meter indicates 12.5 ohms resistance, and since is within acceptable limits of the calculated 12.2 ohms value, it is considered "good".

, To determine the resistance value for a good element, use this online calculator. Provide the voltage (likely 240)and wattage (likely in the range of 1000 to 5000) ratings from the nameplate and click on the "calculate button".

The picture displays the "nameplate" information of the water heater. Two different wattage ratings are provided (4500 / 4500 and 3500 / 3500). The "4500 / 4500" rating is the wattage rating for the upper and and lower element respectively, when connected to a 240 volt supply. Alternatively, the "3500 / 3500" rating is the wattage of the upper and lower element respectively, when connected to a 208 volt supply. Most residential applications are 240 volts, but 208 volt and 120 volt types are also found.

, Prepare the meter by setting the meter to highest resistance scale., The analog meter pointer should move fully to the lowest values of the resistance scale (to the right). The digital meter should indicate a "low" value very close to zero. Locate the "Zero Adjust" knob and turn so that the the meter indicates "0" (or as close to "0" as possible). The digital meter may not have this Zero Adjust feature. Note that this position is the "short circuit" or "zero ohms" indication for this particular resistance range of this meter. Always "zero" the meter when changing resistance ranges., Press the black probe firmly against the metal tank or the heating element mounting nuts or screws (not the other terminal screw). Scrape the metal to ensure a good contact. The meter should display the "infinity" indication as described above in the meter setup. If the meter displays a value other than a very high value (millions of ohms), preferably infinity, the element should be replaced, described later.,,

Lower access panels removed, exposing the plastic shock protector:

,,, If the tank is cold or very hot, it may be difficult to get the expected changes when dialing in different temperatures on the thermostat., The rest of the steps have power on for continued testing. Use extreme caution as the risk of shock injury is greatly increased if not fully paying attention. ,, As mentioned earlier, common voltages for residential (and mobile home / RV) type water heaters are: 120, 208 & 240, with the most common being the 240 volt variety. When steps below discuss measuring "line voltage", substitute the voltage for your particular water heater instead., Line voltage is likely 120, 208 or 240 in the U.S.

, It is a button either red or black, located directly above the thermostat. It most likely has "RESET" printed on it. With a screwdriver or pencil, GENTLY but firmly press in. More on this, later.,,, Check the heater's "field wiring compartment" (usually located on the top of the heater), along the length of the cable that feeds the heater, and lastly inside the electrical panel. Remember, unless power is shut off at panel, this circuit is live at some point between the fuse or circuit breaker and the water heater. Tighten any and all wirenuts and connections in the wiring compartment and inside any junction boxes between here and all the way back to the terminals of the circuit breaker or fuses in the electrical panel. Replace open fuses or reset any tripped circuit breaker if off. Check for power at fuse or circuit breaker. A circuit breaker that immediately trips after resetting indicates a short circuit or less likely, defective circuit breaker., Read the rest of this step slowly and carefully (and repeatedly if needed) until it makes sense as it is the "why and how" the thermostats work together. The key is to understand how the two thermostats interact and function differently. The top thermostat has 2 positions - it can switch voltage to "one position or the other": (position 1) to the top element or (position 2) to the lower thermostat. The lower thermostat also has 2 positions but it is "on and off", not one or the other like the top thermostat: (position 1) to the lower element or (position 2) prevent voltage from reaching the lower element or anywhere else for that matter. In order for the top element to have voltage and heat the water, the temperature of the water in the top of the tank must be lower than the temperature setting of the top thermostat. Once the water in the top part of the tank has reached the temperature value determined by the top thermostat setting, the top thermostat (is considered "satisfied") switches power from the top element to the lower thermostat. If the temperature of the water in the bottom part of the tank is higher than the lower thermostat's setting, the lower thermostat remains "off" and voltage is prevented from reaching the lower heating element. If however, the temperature of the water in the bottom part of the tank is lower than the setting of the lower thermostat, the lower thermostat switches "on" and sends voltage to the lower heating element (a thermostat that has switched power to a heating element or cooling compressor is said to be "calling") and heats the water. The voltage will remain on the lower element until either (a) the bottom thermostat is satisfied or (b), the top thermostat detects that the temperature of the water in the top of the tank has fallen below the temperature setting of the top thermostat. When this occurs, the top thermostat switches the power from the lower thermostat back to the top heating element. This operation continues until the water in both halves of the tank is equal to the settings of their respective thermostats. Setting the top thermostat higher will not cause the top element to turn on if the water temperature in the top of the tank is already higher than the highest setting of the thermostat. If this conditions exists, no click will be detected when turning the temperature setting up and down. It will be necessary to lower the temperature of the water in the tank. The easiest and quickest way to do this is to allow hot water out of the tank by simply opening a hot water faucet. Cold water will enter the bottom of the tank and mix with the existing hot water in the tank, lowering the overall temperature.,,,,, These will be the common screw terminals. The other screw on the thermostat and heating element will be the power screw terminals. When the water is 120 degrees, just 30 degrees cooler; it takes 10 minutes. The skin of children and infants is more sensitive than that of an adult, and will burn more easily. Because of this, selecting a temperature closer to 120 degrees may be a better choice. Lower temperature settings translate to reduced energy costs, too.,,, For this reason, the water level in the tank must be lowered to a point below the level of the element to be removed (otherwise water will leak out as the element is removed). If removing the lower element or unsure how much to drain, emptying the tank's entire contents will prevent unexpected spilling of water., Open the nearest hot water faucet to relieve vacuum pressure and allow air into the tank as it drains. Connect a garden hose to the drain valve at bottom of the tank and extend the hose to a floor drain or some other point that is lower than the drain valve as the tank will only drain down to the highest point of the hose. Open drain valve at the bottom of the water heater and drain the tank.,,, The first method is by bolts that are installed through holes in a flange around the element. Simply use a socket and ratchet or other suitable wrench to remove the 4 bolts and remove the element. The second method is by a threaded portion of the element body that is found under a hex shaped flange of the element. Usually, a 1-1/2" socket will fit perfectly. If a socket of that size is not available, use of a water heater element wrench, channel-lock style pliers or adjustable wrench will get the job done quickly. Twist the element counter-clockwise until it is loosened enough to spin out completely by hand., It is important that all gasket material, filings, rust etc., be completely removed to reveal as smooth a surface as possible. A wire brush and / or sandpaper rubbed over the opening should make easy work of the job., It would be best to bring along the old element for comparison to the new element. Both the upper and lower elements are the same.,,, Make sure the element is snugged tightly against the tank opening, otherwise water will leak out when the tank is filled and under pressure. it will be best to tighten these bolts the way lug nuts on a tire are tightened - one nut, then the opposite nut, repeat as needed. Do not over tighten., At first, only air will be heard escaping out of the hot water faucet. As the tank continues to fill, the hot water faucet will sputter air and be followed by dirty water. Continue fill the tank until the discharge water from the hot water faucet runs clear and without sputtering (steady stream of water) .,, Tighten as needed to stop the leak and wipe dry. Repeat as necessary. Any leak that can not be stopped will require disassembling and cleaning the tank opening and element to ensure a 100% seal when reinstalled., The heating elements must be completely submerged in water before applying power. Failure to completely fill the tank prior applying power will result in instant heating element burnout and will require replacement - again.,, Start with the lowest level faucets and work up to the highest level faucets. Optionally remove shower head(s) and aerators from sink faucets and spray heads to prevent sediment from clogging them.,,, Label the terminals and the wires by either 1) writing numbers on masking tape and apply to the wires and terminals, or 2) apply color coded tape to wires and terminals, or 3) other identifying means, before disconnecting., No screws are used. To remove the controls after removing wires, lift the steel retaining "fingers" on both sides of the control away from the tank slightly and slip the controls out. Excessive force on the fingers may damage them and prevent the controls from seating properly. A poorly seated control prevents it from properly detecting tank temperature, as it relies on firm physical contact with the tank for heat transfer. Removing the controls from a hot tank and retesting will insure tank temperatures were not causing a normal cutout., It may help to bring along the old controls for comparison to the new controls., Remove any rust, debris and dirt.,,

How to Leave a Review on Amazon

Leave a review on Amazon to help other customers make informed buying decisions. If you've ever purchased an item on Amazon, you can leave a review of the item, even if you bought it used or through a third-party seller. As long as you purchased it on Amazon, you can leave a review.

Find the Item You Want to Review

Sign in to your Amazon account. Find the item you want to review by using the search bar at the top of the page. If you can't remember the name of the item, type in the URL of the product page.

Find the Reviews Section

Click on the item you want to review. This brings you to the product page. On the right side of the page, scroll down to the "Customer Reviews" section.

Click on "Write a Review."

Write Your Review

Click on the stars to leave a rating. Leave a title for your review. Write your review in the text field. Consider what you want to say about the item. Be as specific as possible.

Add Photos to Your Review

Click on "Add Photos" to add photos of the item. You can either drag and drop photos from your computer or click on the "Upload" button to locate the photo files on your computer.

Yes, I want to receive feedback!

Don't see the option to write a review? Make sure that you're signed in to your account and that you've purchased the item in the last 90 days. If you've never written a review before, you might need to leave feedback on one item before you can review other items.

Some aesthetic changes will clash against each other. Even worse, you can run out of space to fit all of the pieces on your car. Run all of the ideas past a car expert before making any financial commitments to them.;

Making a bamboo wind chime is a simple and fun project. You can use any size bamboo you like, although thinner poles work better than thick ones. You'll also need some string or twine and a few small bells or other decorative items to hang from the bottom of your chime.

To make your wind chime, start by cutting your bamboo into three even pieces. The length of these pieces will determine the overall length of your wind chime, so you can make them as long or short as you like. Cut a small hole in the center of one end of each bamboo piece, making sure the holes are aligned.

 threading one end of your string or twine through the holes in two of the bamboo pieces, and tie a knot to secure it. Hang one of the decorative items from the bottom of the string, then thread the other end of the string through the hole in the third bamboo piece and tie another knot to secure it.

 Hang your wind chime in a sunny spot outdoors where it will catch the breeze. The gentle sound of the bells or other decorative items will add a touch of relaxation to your outdoor space.

If you sell anything to a customer who has not physically examined the item, but has seen pictures in a catalogue, been sold an item by telephone or has purchased it from a website, then the distance selling regulations apply to you.;
, DSRs do not apply to auctions, such as you might find on eBay, unless the listing is "Buy It Now". Additionally, the DSRs do not apply to items that are personalised, custom-made or perishable, such as flowers.

, The DSRs clearly state that a customer is entitled to a full refund for any reason if they notify the business within seven working days of receiving their package. The item does not need to be faulty. If the customer wishes to return the item, then the business is legally bound to give a full refund, including any postage that the customer may have paid.

, It is essential that the business makes this condition clear in their policies.

,, Most customers will not attempt to return such items in the current climate of blood borne disease, but should they request a full refund the business is legally obliged to give it. The pierced item can not be sold as new once it is returned, and should it be contaminated and sold again, the business will be liable. This part of the law is (as of 2011) under review in the European Courts, but amendments are unlikely to be verified before 2014.

,, If this information is absent from your site, you may find yourself explaining this lack to Trading Standards or other legal bodies.

, The best way to keep your customers happy and abide by the distance selling regulations is to print out your policies and business details, and include them in the dispatched parcel. This will help ensure your customer has all the information about their rights that they need.

Learning to manage people is an essential skill for anyone looking to become a manager or advance their career. While it can be daunting to take on a leadership role, with the right approach, anyone can learn to effectively manage a team.

First, it's important to understand the basics of managing people. This includes setting clear goals and expectations for your team, communicating effectively, and providing feedback and support to help team members succeed. It's also important to be able to delegate tasks and make decisions, as well as to build trust and create a positive work environment.

To learn how to manage people effectively, start by setting clear goals for yourself and your team. This will help you stay focused and on track, and will also help your team understand what they need to do to be successful. Communicate with your team regularly, and provide clear and concise feedback to help them improve. Make sure to also create a positive work environment, and show your team that you're there to support them.

With the right approach, anyone can learn to manage people. By setting clear goals, communicating effectively, and providing support and feedback, you can help your team succeed and reach their full potential.

How to Lattice a Pie

A lattice crust is a woven top crust that is usually used for sweet pies, such as apple or blueberry. Lattices are more visually interesting than a regular pie crust, and provide a better balance between the filling and the crust. They are also much easier to make than a regular pie crust. This article will show you how to lattice a pie.

Step 1
Print out a lattice crust pattern, or draw one on a piece of paper. You can find a basic pattern online.

Step 2
Place the pie crust dough on a lightly floured surface. Roll it out into a square shape that is roughly 1/8-inch thick.

Step 3
Use a knife to cut the dough into strips. There should be eight strips in total, each one roughly 1/2-inch wide.

Step 4
Take four of the strips and place them on top of the pie filling. 

Step 5
Fold back every other strip, then place the remaining four strips on top of the pie filling, perpendicular to the first set of strips. 

Step 6
Unfold the folded strips. 

Step 7
Use a fork to gently seal the strips together.

How to Maintain a Tankless Water Heater

A tankless water heater is a great investment because it will provide you with an endless supply of hot water and it will save you space and money.

The maintenance of a tankless water heater is essential to ensure that it will last long and function well. The most important thing to remember when maintaining a tankless water heater is to flush the tank at least once every year. This will help to remove any build-up of minerals and sediment that may be in the tank.

To flush the tank, you will need to connect a hose to the drain valve and run it outside or into a bucket. Once the hose is connected, open the valve and allow the water to flow out. You should continue to do this until the water runs clear.

It is also important to clean the filter screen on a regular basis. The filter screen is located at the end of the hose that is connected to the tank. To clean it, simply remove the hose and rinse the screen in hot water.

If you notice that your tankless water heater is not working properly, it is important to contact a professional immediately. They will be able to diagnose the problem and make the necessary repairs.

Apple juice and yeast are the only essential ingredients, but there are a few other ingredients that can be added in order to alter the final product.

You can either use apple juice or apple cider. Many prefer the taste of pasteurized, sweet apple cider, but you could use fresh apple juice just as easily. Note, however, that unpasteurized juice will need to be sanitized before you can ferment it.
Dry wine yeasts are affordable and effective. Beer yeasts are also affordable, and many beginning cider makers find them easier to work with. Specialized yeast packages specifically labeled for use with hard apple cider are also available but tend to be more expensive.
Pectic enzyme helps clear cloudiness and is often used with heat-brewed cider.
Adding sugar to the cider will product a higher alcohol content.
The extra apple juice is only needed if you plan to use a starter.;
, All of your supplies need to be washed with hot water and mild dish detergent before use.

Rinse well to remove any soapy residue.
Let the supplies dry completely before use.
Wild bacteria can cause the cider to taste bad and can even turn your batch of hard cider into vinegar.

,

Pour your apple juice into a resealable container with a tight lid.
Pour half of your yeast into the apple juice, seal the container, and shake well for a few seconds.
When bubbling occurs, release the pressure inside the container by opening the lid. Note that it may take five to six hours before bubbling starts.
Reseal the lid and place the container in the refrigerator overnight.
Remove the starter a few hours before you brew your cider.

, Using heat to brew your hard cider is the safest, most common method.

If starting with unpasteurized juice from an unknown source or juice from imperfect apples, the heat method is the safest one to use.
Heat does reduce the aroma of the cider, however. The heat is also more likely to lead to a cloudy cider.

, Place the saucepan of cider on the stove and heat over medium.

Do not allow the juice to boil. The high temperatures required for boiling to occur will cause the pectins in the apple juice or apple cider to set. As a result, you will end up with a cloudier cider.
Monitor the heat of the juice using a food-grade thermometer that clips onto the side of your saucepan. Make sure that the thermometer take the temperature of the liquid, not the temperature of the side or bottom of the pan.

, Once the juice or cider reaches this temperature, cover the saucepan with a lid and let sit for 10 minutes.

Peek at the juice through the lid frequently to make sure that it has not begun to boil.

, Fill a large tub or container with cold water and ice. Place the covered saucepan in the ice water to quickly bring down the temperature.

Your juice is ready for the next part of the process once it reaches 70 degrees Fahrenheit (20 degrees Celsius).

, This is an alternate brewing method and should be used if you do not intend to heat your juice before fermenting it. You should not use both brewing methods.

Sulfites come in the form of campden tablets.
The sulfites are fairly mild and are usually neutralized by the time the cider is ready to enjoy.
You will need to let the juice sit for a while after adding the sulfites in order to reduce the strength of the sulfites. Otherwise, the sulfites may kill some of the yeast.

, Pour the juice or cider directly into the container you intend to ferment the cider in.

A professional-grade fermenter leaves the smallest room for error, but you can use five 1-gallon (4-L) empty milk jugs for a cheaper option.

, Use the back of a spoon to crush the tablets into a fine dust.

Crush the tablets on a plate or other surface that you can easily remove the powder from.

, Add the crushed campden tablets to your juice or cider and stir slightly with a spoon to mix., Cover the fermenter and allow the sulfites to sit in the juice for a full two days before you ferment the cider.

You will ferment the juice with remnants of the sulfites in it. These sulfites will not get filtered out.

, Bring the water to a boil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Turn the heat off once the water boils.

You only need to boil the water if you plan on using a yeast nutrient, and you only need to use a yeast nutrient if you do not plan to use a starter. A starter essentially serves the same purpose as the yeast nutrient, so you only need to use one or the other.

, Stir the yeast nutrient into the hot water until dissolved. Let the water cool until it reaches a range between 80 and 100 degrees Fahrenheit (27 and 38 degrees Celsius).

Note that it is perfectly normal for the mixture to smell bad after you add the yeast nutrient.

, If desired, stir the pectic enzyme into the mixture once it drops to room temperature.

Pectic enzyme is used to clear cloudiness.
If using a starter instead of the yeast nutrient, combine the pectic enzyme with the starter just before adding the starter to the fermenter. Make sure that the starter is at room temperature.

, Pour your juice into the fermenter if it is not already inside. Add the nutrient mixture or starter to the juice and stir slightly to combine.

A professional-grade fermenter may work best, but you could use five 1-gallon (4-L) empty milk jugs for a cheaper option.
Make sure to leave at least 2 inches (5 cm) of empty headspace at the top of the fermenter.

, The sugar is not necessary, but adding sugar to the juice before you ferment it will create a cider with a higher alcohol content.

Moreover, hard cider with sugar in it will continue to improve the longer it ages.

, Gently stir any remaining yeast into your cider.,

Fit the airlock into the top of the fermenter.
Note that instead of an airlock, you could use a piece of plastic wrap stretched over the top of the fermenter and held in place with a rubber band. This method does not work quite as well, but is usually effective.

, Place the cider out of direct sunlight in an area that reaches between 65 and 75 degrees Fahrenheit (18 and 24 degrees Celsius).

The cider should ferment over the next two weeks. During this time, expect to see activity through the clear sides of the airlock.
Once you no longer see any activity in the airlock, wait another 3 to 5 days before bottling the cider.

, This is not absolutely essential, but it is the best way to verify that the fermentation process has been completed.

The hard cider should also taste quite dry.

, Your hard cider is ready to bottle as soon as the fermentation process is finished.

Attach a plastic spigot to the fermenter if it does not already have one on it. Fix food-grade tubing to this spigot, and pour the cider directly into clean, food-grade bottles.
Seal the bottles.
Within two weeks, the cider should be ready to drink.

You can create a stop motion animation with Microsoft Paint and Windows Movie Maker.

 Paintings are a great way to express creativity and tell stories. You can also use paint to create animation! With the help of Microsoft Paint and Windows Movie Maker, you can easily create a stop motion animation.

Plan your animation.

Stop motion animation is a series of still images played in succession to create the illusion of movement. You can create a stop motion animation with as little as two images, but it will look better with more. There is no limit to how many images you can use, but keep in mind that the file size will increase with the number of images.

To plan your animation, draw or sketch the first image on a piece of paper. This will be the starting point for your animation.

Draw or sketch the rest of the images that will make up your animation. These images should be drawn in the same style and size as the first image.

When you have all the images drawn, you can begin to create your animation in Microsoft Paint.

Create your animation in Microsoft Paint.

Open Microsoft Paint by clicking the Start button and then clicking All Programs > Accessories > Paint.

When Paint opens, click the Create a new image button.

In the Create New Image window, enter the desired width and height for your animation in pixels.

Note that the size of your animation will affect the quality of the images. The larger the size, the higher the quality, but the larger the file size will be.

Click OK to create your new image.

On the Tools menu, click the Down Arrow next to the Pen tool to select the Eraser tool.

Use the Eraser tool to remove the background of your animation.

If you want to keep the background, you can skip this step.

Click the Down Arrow next to the Eraser tool to select the Pen tool.

Use the Pen tool to draw the first image of your animation on the new image you just created.

When you are finished drawing the first image, click the Undo button to go back to the previous image.

Draw the rest of the images that will make up your animation, one at a time, using the Pen tool.

When you have finished drawing all the images, you can save the image file.

Save the image file.

On the File menu, click Save.

In the Save As window, enter a name for your image file and click Save.

Repeat this process for each image in your animation.

Create your animation in Windows Movie Maker.

Windows Movie Maker is a free video editing program that comes with Windows. You can use it to create a stop motion animation with your image files.

Open Windows Movie Maker.

On the Start menu, click All Programs > Windows Movie Maker.

When Movie Maker opens, click the Create tab.

In the New Movie dialog box, enter a name for your animation and click Create.

Add the image files to Movie Maker.

On the Home tab, under Add Pictures, click Add Videos and Photos.

In the Add Pictures window, select the image files you created with Microsoft Paint and click Add.

The images will be added to the storyboard in Movie Maker.

 Rearrange the images.

If the images are not in the correct order, you can rearrange them.

In the Storyboard, select the image you want to move.

On the Home tab, under Edit, click Cut.

Click the image you want the selected image to come before.

On the Home tab, under Edit, click Paste.

When you are finished rearranging the images, click the Play button to preview your animation.

If you are not satisfied with the way it looks, you can make changes to the images or the order in which they are played.

When you are happy with the preview, you can publish your animation.

Publish your animation.

On the Home tab, under Share, click Output Settings.

In the Output Settings dialog box, select the format you want to publish your animation in and click Create Movie.

In the Create Movie dialog box, enter a name for your animation and click Create.

Windows Movie Maker will publish your animation.

When it is finished, you can play your animation by clicking the Play button in the Preview section of the Home tab.

How to Restore Oak Cabinets
Restoring oak cabinets is a multi-step process that can be tedious, but the end result is well worth the effort. Before you begin, you should always clean the cabinets with a mixture of dish soap and water, and then rinse them off with plain water. Next, you should determine whether or not you need to sand the cabinets. If there is a clear finish on the cabinets, you will not need to sand, but if there is a varnish or shellac finish, you will need to sand them down before proceeding.

Once you have determined whether or not you need to sand the cabinets, you should start by sanding them down with a fine-grit sandpaper. This will help to remove any dirt or grime that may be on the cabinets, as well as any old finish that may be remaining. Once you have sanded the cabinets, you should wipe them down with a damp cloth to remove any dust.

Next, you should apply a layer of paint or stain to the cabinets. If you are painting the cabinets, you should use a paintbrush to apply a layer of paint to the cabinets, and then use a clean paintbrush to smooth out any brush strokes. If you are staining the cabinets, you should use a cloth to apply a layer of stain to the cabinets, and then use a clean cloth to wipe off any excess stain.

Once you have applied the paint or stain to the cabinets, you should let it dry for a few hours before applying a clear finish to the cabinets. The clear finish will help to protect the paint or stain, and will also give the cabinets a glossy, finished look.

How to Pack for an International Backpacking Trip

Backpacking across Europe, Asia, or another continent is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. However, packing for such a trip can be a challenge, especially if you're trying to pack light. It's essential to pack the right items to ensure that you have a safe, comfortable, and enjoyable trip. This guide will help you determine what to pack and how to pack it.

Determine what you need to pack. Start by making a list of all the essential items you need for your trip. This list should include items such as passport, tickets, travel insurance, credit cards, and any necessary visas or vaccinations.

Choose your backpack. It's important to select a backpack that is comfortable, durable, and has enough space to fit all of your essentials. Consider factors such as size, material, and padding when choosing your backpack.

Pro tip: It's a good idea to pack a small daypack inside your larger backpack for use while exploring during the day.

Lay out all of your gear. Once you have your list of essential items and your backpack, lay out all of the items you plan to bring. This will help you visualize how much you can fit in your backpack and make it easier to pack efficiently.

Prioritize your essentials. As you pack, prioritize the items you need the most, such as your passport, travel insurance, and any necessary visas or vaccinations. Place these items in a separate, easily accessible compartment in your backpack.

Consider packing light. One of the benefits of backpacking is that it allows you to travel light. Consider packing only the essentials, and leave behind any unnecessary items. This will make your backpack lighter and easier to carry, allowing you to enjoy your trip more comfortably.

Use packing cubes. To make the most of your backpack's space, consider using packing cubes. These are small, lightweight bags that can help you organize and compress your clothing, making it easier to fit more into your backpack.

Be aware of your surroundings. As you pack, be mindful of any potential theft or pickpocketing risks. Keep your valuables, such as your passport and credit cards, in a secure location, such as a money belt or a hidden pouch.

Overall, packing for an international backpacking trip requires careful planning and consideration of what you need and don't need. By prioritizing your essentials, packing light, and using packing cubes, you can ensure that you have a safe, comfortable, and enjoyable trip.

How to Plant a Palm Tree

Palm trees are an easy and rewarding plant to grow, provided that you live in a climate that's warm enough to support them. If you do, then there's nothing to stop you from adding a touch of tropical paradise to your property. Just follow these steps to grow your very own palm tree.

Step 1

Pick a container that's big enough for the tree you're growing. Palm trees have extensive root systems, so the bigger the container, the better. For most indoor containers, a diameter of 24 to 36 inches should be sufficient, and the container should be at least 12 to 60 inches deep. The container must also have holes in the bottom for drainage.

Step 2

Fill the container with a well-drained potting soil mix. The soil should be a good mix of fertile garden soil and either perlite or sand. This will provide good drainage and aeration for the roots. 

Step 3

Water the soil well. Palm trees are used to getting plenty of water, so giving the soil a good soaking will help them to grow better. However, make sure to dump out any excess water that collects in the drainage holes.

Step 4

Put the tree in the middle of the pot on top of the soil. You want to avoid putting the tree too close to the side of the pot, as the roots will have trouble growing if they're too cramped. 

Step 5

Fill the remaining space with more of the potting soil mix. Leave about 1 to 2 inches of space at the top. 

Step 6

Water the tree once more. This will help to settle the soil, and it will also provide the tree with more water to help it grow better.

Step 7

Put the tree in a bright spot. Palm trees are tropical plants, so they need plenty of sunlight. Find a spot in your home that gets a lot of sun, preferably a south-facing window.

Step 8

Maintain the tree. You'll need to water the tree once a week, so check the soil to make sure it's not too dry. You'll also need to clean up any dead leaves that fall off the tree.

Building a house is a big project, but with the right tools and planning, it can be a successful and rewarding experience. Here are some steps to help you get started:

1. Plan your budget and timeline: Before you start building, it's important to set a budget and timeline for the project. This will help you stay within your means and on track to meet your deadlines.

2. Find a building lot: Once you have your budget and timeline set, you need to find a building lot that is within your budget and meets your needs.

3. Get permits and approvals: Depending on the location of your building lot, you may need to get permits and approvals from the local government or other authorities.

4. Develop a floor plan: Once you have your permits and approvals, you can start developing a floor plan for your new home. This plan will include the layout of the rooms, the size of the rooms, and the location of the windows and doors.

5. Frame the house: Once the floor plan is complete, you can start framing the house. This involves building the walls, floors, and roof of the house.

6. Install utilities: Once the framing is complete, you can start installing utilities such as plumbing, electricity, and heating and cooling systems.

7. Finish the interior and exterior: Once the utilities are installed, you can start finishing the interior and exterior of the house. This involves installing drywall, painting, and adding any final touches such as landscaping and fencing.

8. Inspect and move in: Once the house is finished, you will need to have it inspected by a professional to ensure that it meets all local building codes and regulations. Once the inspection is complete, you can move into your new home!

How to Make Big Anime Cat Girl Feet for a Cosplay

A quick, easy, and cheap way to make big anime cat girl feet for a cosplay. 

Ingredients:
2 foam mats (1/2" thick)
2 fabric pieces (4" x 6")
2 sheets of pink craft foam
Marking tools
Glue (hot glue gun or liquid craft glue)

Steps:
1. Draw a triangle on the foam mat with the base of the triangle being 4" and the height of the triangle being 3". Cut out the triangle.
2. Draw a half-heart on the foam mat with the straight part of the heart being 3" and the curved part of the heart being 2". Cut out the heart.
3. Draw a small diamond on the foam mat with the sides of the diamond being 1" and the points of the diamond being 1/2". Cut out the diamond.
4. Draw two half-circles on the foam mat with the flat part of the half-circle being 1" and the radius of the half-circle being 1/2". Cut out the half-circles.
5. Draw a small triangle on the foam mat with the base of the triangle being 1/2" and the height of the triangle being 1/4". Cut out the triangle.
6. Gather the foam pieces and glue them together in the shape of a foot. The two triangles will be the toes, the heart will be the ball of the foot, and the two half-circles will be the arch of the foot. Put the pieces together and glue them. Be sure to leave a hole at the bottom of the foot.
7. Stuff the foot with newspaper or other stuffing material.
8. Sew the two fabric pieces together, leaving an opening in one side.
9. Turn the fabric inside out so that the seams are on the inside of the fabric.
10. Stuff the fabric with the foot inside it.
11. Sew up the opening in the fabric.
12. Cut four small holes in the fabric, two on each side of the foot.
13. Sew a piece of string through the holes to create a strap to hold the foot up.
14. Wear the foot with the strap around your ankle.

How to Protect Your PC Against a Virus

If you're using a Windows PC, then you are at risk from viruses. Even Mac and Linux systems are vulnerable, though, so don't think you're safe just because you're not using Windows. The best way to protect your PC is to take a multi-layered approach. That way, if one defense fails, you have others in place to keep you safe. 

Anti-Virus Software

The first line of defense is to install anti-virus software. This software will help to detect and remove viruses from your system. It's important to keep this software up-to-date, as new viruses are released all the time. You should also run regular scans of your system to check for any potential threats.

Be Careful What You Download

Don't download files from untrusted sources. This is one of the most common ways that viruses are spread. If you're not sure if a file is safe, don't download it.

Use a Firewall

A firewall is a piece of software or hardware that helps to block unwanted incoming network traffic. This can help to prevent viruses from getting into your system. Windows has a built-in firewall, but you can also use a third-party firewall.

Keep Your System Up-to-Date

Regularly update your operating system and software. This will help to close any potential security holes that could be used by viruses to get into your system.

Don't Click on Links or Download Attachments from Untrusted Sources

Don't click on links or download attachments from untrusted sources. This is one of the most common ways that viruses are spread. If you're not sure if a link or attachment is safe, don't click on it or download it.

By following these simple steps, you can help to protect your PC from viruses.

How to Draw a Circle in GIMP

The GIMP is a free, open-source image editor that can be used to create and edit graphics, such as circles. Here's how to draw a circle in GIMP:

8. Click on the new path in the Paths window to select it.
9. Click on the Edit Path button in the Paths window.
10. Click on the Start Point icon in the Edit Path window.
11. Drag the start point to the desired location.
12. Hold the Shift key and drag the start point to adjust the shape of the circle.
13. Click on the End Point icon in the Edit Path window.
14. Drag the end point to the desired location.
15. Hold the Shift key and drag the end point to adjust the shape of the circle.

17. Click the Path to Selection button in the Paths window.
18. In the Toolbox window, select the Fill Tool.
19. Click on the color square next to the Fill Tool to select a fill color.

21. Click on the Select Tool in the Toolbox window.
22. Click on the unfilled area outside the circle to deselect it.
23. Save the image by clicking File > Save.

The kinesthetic sense is one of the five senses that the brain uses to perceive the environment. The kinesthetic sense is also called the sense of movement. The brain uses the kinesthetic sense to perceive the position and movement of the body and to navigate through the environment.

Many people are not fully aware of their kinesthetic sense. This is because the brain is often focused on other senses, such as the sense of sight or the sense of sound. However, the kinesthetic sense is an important sense that can provide a lot of information about the environment.

There are many ways to increase your awareness of your kinesthetic sense. One way is to practice meditation. Meditation can help to clear your mind and focus your attention on your body. As you meditate, you can become more aware of your body and its movements.

There are many different types of meditation that can help to increase your kinesthetic awareness. One type of meditation is called mindfulness meditation. This type of meditation involves focusing your attention on your breath and simply observing your body and its movements. Another type of meditation is called body-based meditation. This type of meditation involves focusing your attention on specific parts of your body, such as your hands or your feet.

As you meditate, you may notice that your kinesthetic sense becomes more acute. You may be able to perceive more subtle movements or sensations in your body. As you become more aware of your kinesthetic sense, you may be able to use it to navigate through your environment more effectively.
